name: Abridge
classification_notes: A geometric sans drawn on a slightly condensed frame, with rounded terminals and a humanist rhythm in the lowercase. Sits between a workhorse text sans and a friendly display face.
summary: A six-weight geometric sans with rounded terminals, useful for branding, editorial and interface work where a softer voice is wanted.
description: A geometric sans by atipo foundry that runs from Thin through ExtraLight, Light, Regular and Medium up to Bold. The lowercase has a humanist rhythm and rounded terminals that give it a warmer voice than a pure geometric grotesque, while the uppercase keeps a tidy circular structure. Numeric typography is well covered with pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscripts, scientific inferiors and ordinals, and Latin Extended language support reaches across European, Pacific and indigenous American languages.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Abridge as part of their pay-what-you-want catalogue, released with six weights from Thin to Bold and a feature set covering fractions, superiors and ordinals for numeric typography. They list very broad Latin Extended language coverage, including Afrikaans, Welsh, Quechua, Hawaiian and Tagalog among many others.

name: Alcyone
classification_notes: A humanist sans with a calligraphic skeleton, modulated stroke and a true italic. Built as a workhorse text family with nine weights and matching italics across the range.
summary: A humanist sans family from Thin to Black with matching italics, drawn for long-form text and data-heavy layouts.
description: A humanist sans by atipo foundry built around a calligraphic skeleton and a slight stroke contrast. The family runs from Thin through ExtraLight, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold up to Black, with a true italic for every weight, giving sixteen styles in total. OpenType features include tabular figures for tables, price lists and financial reports, plus pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. Latin Extended language coverage is very wide, reaching across European, Pacific and indigenous American languages.
foundry_blurb: atipo position Alcyone as part of their pay-what-you-want catalogue, released with eight weights from Thin to Black plus matching italics, tabular figures, fractions and ordinals, and language support that ranges from Afrikaans and Welsh through to Quechua, Hawaiian and Tagalog.

name: Archia
classification_notes: A geometric sans with single-storey a, low contrast and a neutral, slightly technical voice. Built for branding, signage and interface use rather than long-form text.
summary: A six-weight geometric sans with a single-storey a, drawn for branding, signage and interface work.
description: A geometric sans by atipo foundry with a single-storey a, low stroke contrast and a tidy, slightly technical voice. The family runs through six weights from Thin and Light to Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold. OpenType features include tabular figures for tables and financial layouts, plus pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. Language coverage takes in the main Latin scripts of Western, Central and Northern Europe.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Archia as a pay-what-you-want family with six weights from Thin to Bold, tabular figures and a full set of fraction, superior and ordinal features. The page lists Latin language coverage across Albanian, Basque, Croatian, Czech, Welsh and other major European languages.

name: Argesta
classification_notes: A high-contrast didone-flavoured serif drawn as an optical-size system, with a hairline cut for very large display, headline and display weights for editorial use, and a Text cut tuned for body copy.
summary: A didone-flavoured serif system with hairline, headline, display and text optical sizes, designed for editorial and fashion-leaning work.
description: A high-contrast serif by atipo foundry built as an optical-size system. Argesta Hairline is drawn for very large sizes, Argesta Headline and Argesta Display sit at editorial display sizes, and Argesta Text is tuned for body copy with a Bold companion. Each cut has a matching italic, and the family includes oldstyle and lining figures, standard and discretionary ligatures, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms and pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. Latin language coverage takes in the main European languages.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Argesta as a serif drawn across optical sizes, with Hairline, Headline, Display and Text cuts plus italics and a Text Bold. They highlight oldstyle and lining figures, standard and discretionary ligatures, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms and a complete fraction, superior and ordinal set.

name: Babcock
classification_notes: A modern serif with sharp wedge serifs, vertical stress and a generous lowercase. Sits between a contemporary text serif and an editorial display face.
summary: A four-weight modern serif with sharp wedge serifs, drawn for editorial and brand work that wants a contemporary tone.
description: A modern serif by atipo foundry with sharp wedge serifs, vertical stress and a generous lowercase. The family runs through four upright weights, Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold, and includes pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals for numeric typography. Latin Extended language coverage is very wide, reaching across European, Pacific and indigenous American languages.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Babcock as part of their pay-what-you-want catalogue, with four upright weights from Regular to Bold and a numeric typography set covering fractions, superiors and ordinals. The language list runs from Afrikaans and Welsh through to Quechua, Hawaiian and Tagalog.

name: Bariol
classification_notes: A slightly rounded humanist sans with open apertures, soft terminals and a friendly tone. Designed as a workhorse text family with eight styles and a true italic.
summary: A friendly rounded humanist sans in four weights with italics, paired with a serif and an icon companion across the wider Bariol system.
description: A humanist sans by atipo foundry with softly rounded terminals, open apertures and a warm tone. The family runs through Thin, Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics for eight styles in total. OpenType features include standard and discretionary ligatures, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms, lining, tabular and oldstyle figures, plus pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. Bariol sits at the centre of a wider system that also includes Bariol Serif and Bariol Icons. Language coverage runs across the main European Latin scripts.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Bariol as one of their flagship pay-what-you-want releases, offered with thin, light, regular and bold weights plus italics, alongside a serif and an icon companion. They highlight ligatures, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms and a full lining, tabular and oldstyle figure set, with language coverage spanning Western and Central European Latin scripts.

name: Bariol Icons
classification_notes: A pictographic companion to Bariol with 52 icons drawn in the same rounded humanist style. Used as an interface and editorial pairing with the Bariol type system.
summary: A 52-icon companion to the Bariol type system, drawn in the same rounded humanist style for use in interfaces and editorial layouts.
description: An icon font by atipo foundry that pairs with the Bariol family. The set contains 52 pictograms drawn in the same rounded humanist style as the type, designed to slot into running text, interface labels and editorial layouts. As a symbol font it has a single regular cut and is intended to be used together with Bariol or Bariol Serif rather than as a standalone text family.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Bariol Icons as a pay-what-you-want set of 52 icons that completes the wider Bariol system, sitting alongside the sans and serif families.

name: Bariol Serif
classification_notes: A softly rounded humanist serif drawn as a serif companion to Bariol. Open apertures, gentle bracketed serifs and a warm tone for editorial text and branding.
summary: A softly rounded humanist serif in four weights with italics, drawn as the serif companion to Bariol.
description: A humanist serif by atipo foundry that pairs with the Bariol sans. The family runs through Thin, Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics, giving eight styles in total. OpenType features include standard and discretionary ligatures, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms, lining, tabular and oldstyle figures, and a full set of pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. Latin language coverage runs across the main European scripts.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Bariol Serif as the serif companion to Bariol, with thin, light, regular and bold weights plus italics, ligatures, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms and lining, tabular and oldstyle figures.

name: Barnegat
classification_notes: A single-style decorative display face with a strong illustrative voice, drawn for posters, packaging and editorial titles rather than continuous text.
summary: A single-style decorative display face for posters, packaging and editorial titles.
description: A display face by atipo foundry drawn as a single decorative cut. The page lists one style alongside numeric features, including pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. It is intended for posters, packaging and editorial titles rather than long-form running text. Language coverage takes in the main European Latin scripts.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Barnegat as a pay-what-you-want display face with a single style, listing fractions, superiors and ordinals among its OpenType features and language coverage across the main European Latin scripts.

name: Basier
classification_notes: A neo-grotesque sans drawn in two parallel cuts: Basier Circle with rounded terminals and Basier Square with squared terminals. Both run from Regular to Bold with italics, sharing the same metrics so they can be mixed in the same layout.
summary: A neo-grotesque sans drawn in parallel Circle and Square cuts, with mono and narrow companions for a full system.
description: A neo-grotesque sans by atipo foundry built as two parallel cuts: Basier Circle, with softly rounded terminals, and Basier Square, with squared terminals. Each runs through Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold with matching italics, sharing metrics so the cuts can be mixed in the same layout. OpenType features include stylistic alternates and a full set of fractions, superiors and ordinals. The wider Basier system extends into Basier Mono and Basier Narrow, giving a coordinated toolkit for branding, interface and product work.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Basier as a sans drawn in two complementary cuts, Circle and Square, each with regular, medium, semibold and bold weights plus italics. They list stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals, and frame Mono and Narrow as part of the same Basier system.

name: Basier Mono
classification_notes: The monospaced cut of the Basier system, drawn in Circle and Square variants so it can pair with the proportional Basier in the same layout. Built for code, technical writing and interface work.
summary: The monospaced companion to Basier, drawn in Circle and Square cuts for code, technical writing and interface work.
description: A monospaced sans by atipo foundry that completes the Basier system. It is drawn in two parallel cuts, Mono Circle and Mono Square, each running from Regular through Medium and SemiBold up to Bold with matching italics. OpenType features include stylistic alternates and a full set of fractions, superiors and ordinals. Designed to pair with the proportional Basier in the same layout, it is well suited to code, technical writing, dashboards and product interfaces.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Basier Mono as the monospaced cut of the Basier system, offered in Circle and Square variants with regular, medium, semibold and bold weights plus italics, alongside stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals.

name: Basier Narrow
classification_notes: The narrow cut of the Basier system, drawn in Circle and Square variants so it can sit alongside the proportional Basier and Basier Mono. Built for tight measures, mastheads and dense interface layouts.
summary: A narrow cut of Basier in Circle and Square variants, for tight measures, mastheads and dense interface layouts.
description: A narrow neo-grotesque sans by atipo foundry that extends the Basier system into tighter widths. It is drawn in two parallel cuts, Narrow Circle and Narrow Square, each running through Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold with matching italics. OpenType features include stylistic alternates and a full set of fractions, superiors and ordinals. Suited to mastheads, navigation, data tables and any layout where horizontal space is at a premium while a Basier-family voice is wanted.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Basier Narrow as the narrow cut of the Basier system, offered in Circle and Square variants with regular, medium, semibold and bold weights plus italics, stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals.

name: Borna
classification_notes: A humanist sans with a calligraphic skeleton, slight stroke contrast and a true italic. Drawn for branding and editorial work where a warmer, more personable voice is wanted than a pure grotesque.
summary: A humanist sans in four weights with italics, drawn for branding and editorial work that wants a warmer, more personable tone.
description: A humanist sans by atipo foundry with a calligraphic skeleton and slight stroke contrast. The family runs through Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold with matching italics for eight styles in total. OpenType features include tabular figures for tables, price lists and financial reports, plus pre-designed fractions, numerators, denominators, superscript, scientific inferiors and ordinals. Language coverage runs across the main European Latin scripts.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Borna as a pay-what-you-want humanist sans with regular, medium, semibold and bold weights plus italics, tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals, and language coverage across the main European Latin scripts.

name: Bould
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A rounded geometric sans by atipo, sitting between geometric and humanist with soft terminals and even stroke weight. Designed for screen and identity work where a contemporary, friendly tone is wanted.
summary: A rounded geometric sans from atipo with seven weights and matching italics, built for warm, contemporary identity and interface work.
description: A geometric sans by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry. Bould runs across seven weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, with even stroke weight, soft terminals and tabular figures, fractions and stylistic alternates available through OpenType features. The pay-what-you-want model makes it a common choice for identity and screen work that wants a friendly, geometric tone without the over-familiarity of Circular or Avenir.
foundry_blurb: atipo release Bould as part of their pay-what-you-want catalogue, presenting it as a geometric sans family with seven weights and italics. The page highlights stylistic alternates, tabular figures and fractions, and lists Latin Extended language support across European languages.

name: Brockmann
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A neo-grotesque sans named for Josef Muller-Brockmann, drawing on Swiss modernist sans serifs in the lineage of Helvetica and Akzidenz Grotesk. Built for editorial and identity work that wants a quiet, rational tone.
summary: A neo-grotesque sans by atipo named for Josef Muller-Brockmann, sitting in the Swiss modernist tradition with four weights and italics.
description: A neo-grotesque sans by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, named for Swiss designer Josef Muller-Brockmann. Brockmann offers four weights from Regular to Bold with matching italics, with stylistic alternates, tabular figures and fractions in the OpenType layer. The drawing leans Swiss and rational rather than humanist, sitting close to Helvetica and Akzidenz Grotesk in tone, and is positioned for editorial systems and identities that want quiet authority.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Brockmann on their site as a typeface released under their pay-what-you-want model, with eight styles across four weights and italics. The features list covers fractions, tabular figures and stylistic alternates, with Latin Extended language coverage across European scripts.

name: Bulevar
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A high-contrast display family rooted in early twentieth century French and Spanish poster lettering, with a Regular for general display use and a Poster cut for the heaviest contrast.
summary: A high-contrast art deco display family by atipo, with a Regular and Poster cut drawn from early twentieth century European poster lettering.
description: A display family by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, drawing on early twentieth century art deco poster lettering. Bulevar comes in a Regular and a Poster cut where contrast is pushed further, with stylistic alternates, fractions and case-sensitive forms in the OpenType layer. Suited to fashion, beauty and editorial display where a vintage European poster reference is wanted.
foundry_blurb: atipo release Bulevar through their pay-what-you-want store, presenting it as a display family with a Regular and a Poster style. The features list includes stylistic alternates, fractions and case-sensitive forms, with Latin Extended language coverage.

name: Calendas Plus
classification_notes: A transitional serif with elegant proportions, modest contrast and a calligraphic italic. The Plus version expands the original Calendas with small caps, swashes and discretionary ligatures, sitting in the lineage of Caslon and Baskerville with a softer, contemporary touch.
summary: A transitional serif with calligraphic italic, small caps and swashes, suited to editorial, book and elegant identity work.
description: A transitional serif by atipo foundry, expanded as Calendas Plus with small caps, swashes and discretionary ligatures. Offered in Regular, Italic and Bold, the family carries oldstyle and lining figures, fractions and case-sensitive forms through OpenType. Sits in editorial, book and identity work where a quiet, slightly literary serif is wanted, with the swash italic available when display moments need more flourish.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Calendas Plus as a typeface available through their pay-what-you-want model, with Regular, Italic and Bold styles. The features list highlights oldstyle and lining figures, standard and discretionary ligatures, stylistic alternates and swash characters, small caps and case-sensitive forms across broad Latin language support.

name: Cassannet Plus
classification_notes: A geometric art deco display family inspired by the lettering of French poster artist A. M. Cassandre. Six weights from Thin to Ultra carry forward the Cassandre tradition of bold, modernist poster type.
summary: A geometric art deco display family by atipo inspired by A. M. Cassandre, running from Thin to Ultra for poster and identity display work.
description: A geometric display family by atipo foundry inspired by the poster lettering of A. M. Cassandre. Cassannet Plus comes in six weights from Thin to Ultra, with stylistic alternates and discretionary ligatures that allow the type to be packed for poster and editorial display, plus an extended Latin language set. Note the alternates and discretionary ligatures are not included in the heaviest Black and Ultra weights.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Cassannet Plus as part of their pay-what-you-want catalogue, with six weights from Thin to Ultra. The features section highlights stylistic alternates and discretionary ligatures, with the note that those features are not included in the Black and Ultra weights, alongside broad Latin language coverage.

name: Chaney
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A wide industrial sans drawn for display, with four widths from Regular through to Ultra Extended that push horizontal proportions into wood-type territory. Useful for posters, mastheads and identities that want presence and width.
summary: A wide industrial display sans by atipo with four widths from Regular to Ultra Extended, drawn for posters and large-scale display.
description: An industrial display sans by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, designed across four widths: Regular, Wide, Extended and Ultra Extended. Chaney leans into the wood-type tradition with squared terminals and broad horizontals, and includes stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals. Sits well on posters, mastheads, sports identities and any setting where width is the typographic argument.
foundry_blurb: atipo release Chaney through their pay-what-you-want store as a display family across four widths: Regular, Wide, Extended and Ultra Extended. The features list covers stylistic alternates, fractions and superiors, with Latin Extended language support across European scripts.

name: Doumbar
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A geometric display sans built as a system of three subfamilies: Doumbar, Doumbar Plate and Doumbar Stencil. Drawn with strong verticals and circular bowls, useful for identity, signage and editorial display where a constructed feel is wanted.
summary: A geometric display sans system from atipo across three subfamilies, including Plate and Stencil cuts, in four weights for signage and identity work.
description: A geometric display sans by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, structured as three subfamilies: the standard Doumbar, Doumbar Plate with engraved inline detail, and Doumbar Stencil. Each subfamily runs across four weights from Regular to Bold, with fractions, superiors and ordinals in the OpenType layer. The system suits signage, identities and editorial display where you want one geometric voice with multiple textural registers.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Doumbar through their pay-what-you-want catalogue as a system of three subfamilies, Doumbar, Doumbar Plate and Doumbar Stencil, each in four weights from Regular to Bold. The features section includes fractions, superiors and ordinals across Latin Extended language coverage.

name: Geomanist
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A geometric sans with humanist warmth, sitting between Futura and Avenir in tone but with its own slightly softer rhythm. Nine weights from Thin to Ultra with matching italics make it a workhorse for identity, editorial and screen.
summary: A geometric humanist sans by atipo across nine weights with italics, suited to identity, editorial and interface work that needs warmth and range.
description: A geometric humanist sans by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, with nine weights from Thin to Ultra and matching italics. Geomanist carries tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals and case-sensitive forms in its OpenType layer, with broad Latin language coverage. The drawing softens the geometric model with humanist proportions, sitting between Futura and Avenir in tone, and works as a workhorse across identity, editorial and screen.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Geomanist as part of their pay-what-you-want catalogue, with nine weights and italics from Thin to Ultra. The features section highlights tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals and case-sensitive forms, with broad Latin language coverage including Belarusian Lacinka.

name: Gottak
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A humanist sans with calligraphic underpinnings, slight stroke modulation and open apertures. Six weights with matching italics support both display and text use across an unusually broad set of Latin-script languages including indigenous, regional and constructed languages.
summary: A humanist sans by atipo across six weights with italics, with unusually broad Latin language coverage spanning indigenous and regional scripts.
description: A humanist sans by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, with six weights from Thin to Bold and matching italics. Gottak carries stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals and an unusually broad Latin language set covering European, indigenous and constructed languages including Quechua, Hawaiian, Maori, Cherokee, Navajo and many others. Useful where humanist warmth and serious language coverage have to coexist.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Gottak through their pay-what-you-want store, with six weights and italics. The page highlights stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals, and a notably extensive language list spanning European, indigenous American, Pacific and constructed Latin-script languages.

name: Izoard
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A geometric display family with two cuts, Izoard and Izoard Soft, sitting in stencil and rounded territory with a sporting, technical feel. Named for the Col d'Izoard climb in the Alps, it suits sports, outdoor and editorial display.
summary: A geometric stencil display family by atipo with a standard Izoard and a softer Izoard Soft cut, named for the Alpine climb.
description: A geometric stencil display family by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, named for the Col d'Izoard. Two cuts are offered, Izoard and Izoard Soft, with case-sensitive forms, true small caps, fractions, superiors and ordinals in the OpenType layer. Sits well on sports, outdoor, technical and editorial display where you want a constructed, slightly mechanical voice.
foundry_blurb: atipo release Izoard through their pay-what-you-want store as a geometric display family with Izoard and Izoard Soft cuts. The features section covers case-sensitive forms, true small caps, fractions, superiors and ordinals, with Latin Extended language support.

name: Knile
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A contemporary serif with calligraphic underpinnings and modest contrast, sitting in the lineage of post-Garamond text serifs but drawn with cleaner, slightly more geometric proportions. Nine weights with matching italics from Thin to Black support both text and display.
summary: A contemporary text and display serif by atipo across eight weights with italics, drawn for editorial systems that need both text and display range.
description: A contemporary serif by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, with eight weights from Thin to Black and matching italics. Knile includes tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals and case-sensitive forms in its OpenType layer, with broad Latin language coverage including Belarusian Lacinka. Designed to flex across editorial text, branding and display, with calligraphic italics that hold their own as display.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Knile through their pay-what-you-want catalogue, with eight weights and italics from Thin to Black. The features section covers tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals and case-sensitive forms, with Latin Extended language support.

name: MG12
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A humanist grotesque drawn for text and identity, with three weights and italics. The MG12 name nods to the systemic, almost technical character of the family, which carries wide Latin language support across European, indigenous and constructed scripts.
summary: A humanist grotesque from atipo across three weights with italics, with notably broad Latin language coverage for editorial and identity systems.
description: A humanist grotesque by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, with three weights from Regular to Bold and matching italics. MG12 carries tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals in the OpenType layer, and a very broad Latin language set spanning European, indigenous American, Pacific and constructed languages. Useful where a contemporary humanist sans has to operate across many language scripts.
foundry_blurb: atipo release MG12 through their pay-what-you-want store as a humanist grotesque in three weights with italics. The features list covers tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals, alongside an extensive Latin language set covering European, indigenous and constructed scripts.

name: Musetta
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez
classification_notes: A high-contrast display serif with sharp terminals and narrow proportions, drawn in the spirit of fashion-magazine didones. A single Regular weight focused on display use rather than running text.
summary: A high-contrast display serif by atipo in a single Regular cut, drawn in the spirit of fashion-magazine didones for editorial and luxury display.
description: A high-contrast display serif by Raul Garcia del Pomar and Ismael Gonzalez at atipo foundry, offered as a single Regular weight. Musetta sits in didone display territory with sharp terminals, narrow proportions and pronounced thick-thin contrast, and includes stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals in the OpenType layer. Suited to fashion, beauty, editorial and luxury display where a sharp, theatrical serif voice is wanted.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Musetta on their site as part of the pay-what-you-want catalogue, offered as a single Regular weight. The features section covers stylistic alternates, fractions, superiors and ordinals, with Latin Extended language coverage across European scripts.

name: N27
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo describe N27 as their first avant-grotesk family, a neo-grotesque drawn for editorial and identity work with stylistic alternates that switch the look of selected characters.
summary: Atipo's avant-grotesk in six weights with italics, built for editorial and identity work with stylistic alternates on tap.
description: A neo-grotesque sans by atipo described as their first avant-grotesk family. Twelve styles run across six weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, and the family carries stylistic alternates that switch the look of selected characters along with fractions, superiors and ordinals. Latin language support covers the major European scripts. Atipo offer Regular and Italic as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo present N27 as their first avant-grotesk font family, with stylistic alternates and full numeric typography features including fractions, superiors and ordinals.

name: Novela
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo position Novela as a contemporary serif family with a Display cut for headlines and a text cut for running copy, supplied with true small caps and ornaments.
summary: A contemporary serif family from atipo with display and text cuts, true small caps, ornaments and a poster sibling for the largest sizes.
description: A contemporary serif by atipo built for editorial and identity work. Ten styles run across five weights from Display Regular through Regular, Semibold, Bold and Black, each paired with an italic, and the family includes true small caps, oldstyle and lining figures, standard ligatures, stylistic alternates, ornaments and case-sensitive forms. The Display cut sharpens contrast for headlines while the text cut keeps the rhythm warm at body sizes, and Novela Poster extends the system to the largest display settings. Atipo offer the Regular as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Novela as a contemporary serif family with display and text cuts, true small caps, oldstyle and lining figures, standard ligatures, stylistic alternates, ornaments and case-sensitive forms.

name: Novela Poster
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo position Novela Poster as the display extreme of the Novela system, drawn for the largest sizes with the same small caps, ornaments and stylistic alternates as the wider family.
summary: The poster cut of Novela, sharpened for the largest sizes with true small caps, ornaments and the same stylistic alternates as the wider family.
description: A display serif by atipo that takes the Novela system to its sharpest display setting. Two styles, Poster and Poster Italic, sit at the top of the optical system and carry the family's true small caps, oldstyle and lining figures, standard ligatures, stylistic alternates, ornaments and case-sensitive forms. Sits in editorial covers, masthead and identity work where you want the contrast and detail of Novela pushed as far as it will go.
foundry_blurb: Atipo present Novela Poster as the display extreme of the Novela family, sharing the small caps, ornaments and stylistic alternates of the wider system.

name: Noway
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo describe Noway as a functional geometric sans, supplied with tabular figures, a slashed zero and a companion icon set, with a rounded sibling in Noway Round.
summary: A functional geometric sans from atipo with five weights, italics, tabular figures, a slashed zero and a matching icon set.
description: A functional geometric sans by atipo. Ten styles run across five weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, and the family ships with tabular figures, a slashed zero, fractions, superiors and ordinals along with a Noway Icons companion set. Drawn for interface, data and identity work where the rhythm needs to stay calm at small sizes and hold its shape in tables. Atipo offer Regular and Italic as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Noway as a functional sans serif with tabular figures and a slashed zero, full numeric features and a companion icon set.

name: Noway Round
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo describe Noway Round as the rounded sibling of Noway, drawn for friendlier interface and identity work with the same tabular figures, slashed zero and icon companion.
summary: The rounded sibling of Noway, a softer functional geometric sans with five weights, italics, tabular figures and a matching icon set.
description: A rounded geometric sans by atipo, drawn as the softer companion to Noway. Ten styles run across five weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, and the family carries tabular figures, a slashed zero, fractions, superiors and ordinals along with a Noway Round Icons set. Sits in product, interface and identity work where the brand wants warmth without losing the discipline of a functional geometric. Atipo offer Regular and Italic as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Noway Round as a functional rounded font with tabular figures, a slashed zero, full numeric features and a companion icon set.

name: Nudica
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo position Nudica as a stripped back neo-grotesque, paired with Nudica Mono, drawn to capture what they call the naked essence of beauty in everyday text and interface use.
summary: A stripped back neo-grotesque from atipo in six weights with italics, paired with Nudica Mono for code and data work.
description: A neo-grotesque sans by atipo, drawn as a calm workhorse for text, interface and identity. Twelve styles run across six weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, and the family carries tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals. Pairs with Nudica Mono so editorial systems and product UI can move between prose, data and code without breaking voice. Atipo offer Medium as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Nudica as capturing the naked essence of beauty, with tabular figures and full numeric typography features, sitting alongside a matching Nudica Mono.

name: Nudica Mono
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo present Nudica Mono as the fixed-width companion to Nudica, sharing skeleton and weight set so prose, data and code can sit in the same system.
summary: The fixed-width companion to Nudica, a neo-grotesque mono in six weights with italics for code, data and tabular work.
description: A monospaced neo-grotesque by atipo that pairs with the proportional Nudica. Twelve styles run across six weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, sharing skeleton and rhythm with the proportional family while holding a fixed advance for code editors, data tables and console output. Carries fractions, superiors and ordinals so numeric content stays consistent with the wider system. Atipo offer Medium as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Nudica Mono as capturing the naked essence of beauty in monospaced form, with full numeric typography features and a shared skeleton with Nudica.

name: Parking
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo position Parking as a single-weight display drawn from a stencil reading of street and signage lettering, supplied with stylistic alternates and full numeric features.
summary: A single-weight stencil display from atipo with stylistic alternates, drawn from the rhythm of street and signage lettering.
description: A stencil display by atipo, supplied as a single Regular cut. Carries stylistic alternates that switch the look of selected characters along with fractions, superiors and ordinals, and reads as a piece of urban vernacular pulled into a usable typeface. Sits in posters, packaging and identity work that wants the bluntness of street signage without losing legibility.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Parking as offering stylistic alternates and full numeric typography features in a single-weight display cut.

name: Quablo
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo summarise Quablo with the line Form, Function, Future, positioning it as a contemporary geometric sans with seven weights and a wide Latin extended language set.
summary: A contemporary geometric sans from atipo in seven weights with italics, supplied with tabular figures and a wide Latin extended language set.
description: A geometric sans by atipo, summarised by the studio with the line Form, Function, Future. Fourteen styles run across seven weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, and the family carries tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals along with a deep Latin extended language set covering minority and creole scripts alongside the major European languages. Sits in identity, product and editorial work where you want a calm geometric voice with broad language reach. Atipo offer Medium as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Quablo with the tagline Form, Function, Future, supplying tabular figures, full numeric features and a Latin extended character set that covers more than a hundred languages.

name: Rawest
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo describe Rawest as a neo-geometric, summarised by the line The beauty of toughness, supplied as upright cuts in seven weights without italics.
summary: A neo-geometric sans from atipo in seven upright weights, drawn around the idea of the beauty of toughness.
description: A neo-geometric sans by atipo, summarised by the studio as The beauty of toughness. Seven upright weights run from Thin to Bold without italics, and the family carries fractions, superiors and ordinals. Sits in identity, packaging and display work that wants a hard-edged geometric voice without slipping into pure utility. Atipo offer Medium as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Rawest as a Neo-Geometric font around the idea of the beauty of toughness, with full numeric typography features.

name: Rouna
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo describe Rouna as the font around the circle, a rounded geometric sans with three weights, italics, stylistic alternates and tabular figures.
summary: A rounded geometric sans from atipo built around the circle, with three weights, italics, stylistic alternates and tabular figures.
description: A rounded geometric sans by atipo, described by the studio as the font around the circle. Six styles run across three weights, Regular, Medium and Bold, each with a matching italic, and the family carries stylistic alternates, tabular figures, fractions, superiors, ordinals and case-sensitive forms. Sits in identity, packaging and product work where the brand wants warmth and a clear geometric base. Atipo offer Bold as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Rouna as The Font Around the Circle, supplying stylistic alternates, tabular figures, full numeric typography features and case-sensitive forms.

name: Round 8
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo bill Round 8 as their heavyweight rounded display, supplied as eight numbered cuts that read as a graded weight system across a wide Latin extended language set.
summary: Atipo's heavyweight rounded display, supplied as eight numbered cuts that step from light to black across a wide Latin extended language set.
description: A rounded display by atipo, billed by the studio as their heavyweight typeface. The family is supplied as eight numbered cuts, One through Eight, that read as a graded weight system from the lightest to the heaviest end. Carries fractions, superiors and ordinals along with a deep Latin extended character set covering more than a hundred languages. Sits in identity, packaging and editorial work that wants the softness of rounded geometry alongside genuine display heft.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Round 8 as the greatest atipo foundry heavyweight typeface of all time, with full numeric typography features and an extended Latin character set.

name: Safiro
designer: Raul Garcia del Pomar, Ismael Gonzalez (atipo)
classification_notes: Atipo summarise Safiro as the Swiss style carved like a gemstone, a precise neo-grotesque in four weights with italics and tabular figures.
summary: A precise neo-grotesque from atipo summarised as the Swiss style carved like a gemstone, with four weights, italics and tabular figures.
description: A neo-grotesque sans by atipo, summarised by the studio as the Swiss style carved like a gemstone. Eight styles run across four weights, Regular, Medium, Semibold and Bold, each with a matching italic, and the family carries tabular figures, fractions, superiors and ordinals. Sits in editorial, identity and product work that wants Swiss-school discipline with sharper, more cut detail. Atipo offer Medium as a free download with the rest of the family on a pay what you want basis.
foundry_blurb: Atipo describe Safiro as The Swiss style carved like a gemstone, supplying tabular figures and full numeric typography features.

name: Salome
classification_notes: A high-contrast Didone display serif in the Bodoni and Didot lineage. Atipo offer Salome in regular and italic, plus Fine, Stencil and Deco cuts that lean further into editorial display use.
summary: A high-contrast Didone display serif from atipo with Fine, Stencil and Deco cuts for fashion and editorial work.
description: Salome is atipo foundry's high-contrast display serif, sitting in the Didone family alongside Bodoni and Didot. The core family pairs a Regular and an Italic with a thin Fine cut, a Stencil cut and a geometric Deco cut, which gives it the range to run from elegant fashion mastheads to art-directed editorial covers. It includes true small caps, swash characters, stylistic alternates, lining and oldstyle figures, fractions, ordinals and discretionary ligatures, so it earns its keep across long-form layouts as well as logotypes. Latin Extended language support covers most European languages.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Salome as a Didone-style display serif released under their pay-what-you-want model, offered in Regular and Italic with companion Fine, Stencil and Deco cuts. The page documents a full set of OpenType features including standard and discretionary ligatures, swash characters, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms, true small caps, lining and oldstyle figures, fractions, superiors, inferiors and ordinals.

name: Sawton
classification_notes: A geometric sans system in three flavours, Circular, Industrial and Bauhaus, each running from Thin to Bold. The three subfamilies share metrics so they can be mixed across a single layout.
summary: A three-flavour geometric sans system from atipo, offered as Circular, Industrial and Bauhaus cuts across five weights.
description: Sawton is a geometric sans-serif system from atipo foundry built around three sibling cuts. Sawton Circular leans on perfectly circular bowls in the Futura and Avenir tradition, Sawton Industrial squares those forms off for a more mechanical reading, and Sawton Bauhaus pushes the construction toward early twentieth-century geometric experiments. Each subfamily runs from Thin to Bold across five weights with shared metrics, so designers can swap between cuts within a single layout for headline texture. Best used for branding, posters and editorial display where geometric character is doing the talking.
foundry_blurb: atipo describe Sawton as a geometric system organised in three subfamilies, Circular, Industrial and Bauhaus, each available from Thin to Bold. The page shows the three cuts running side by side in the tester, signalling that they are designed to share a baseline so they can be combined in the same composition.

name: Scilla
classification_notes: A modern serif with a regular width and a Narrow companion, each in roman and italic. The contrast and bracketed terminals place it in contemporary editorial serif territory.
summary: A modern serif from atipo offered in a regular width and a Narrow cut, each with a true italic.
description: Scilla is atipo foundry's modern serif, offered in a regular cut and a Narrow companion that drops the proportions for tighter columns and stacked headlines. Both widths ship with a true italic, giving editorial designers a pairing that can carry runs of body copy and shift up into display sizes without losing personality. Sits alongside contemporary editorial serifs and works well in fashion, lifestyle and culture publishing.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Scilla as a serif system with two widths, the standard Scilla and a Scilla Narrow, each available in roman and italic. The tester exposes all four styles, framing the family as an editorial pair rather than a single weight.

name: Sfizia
classification_notes: A transitional serif released with a regular and a bold roman plus two italic variants, the second of which carries swashier display flourishes.
summary: A transitional serif from atipo with a regular, a bold and two italics, including a swashier alternate italic.
description: Sfizia is a transitional serif from atipo foundry that ships with a Regular and a Bold roman plus two italic cuts. The second italic, labelled Italic No. 2, leans into more decorative letterforms with extra flourishes, giving designers a quieter italic for body copy and a more expressive one for display lines and titles. A neat fit for editorial, packaging and identity work that wants the calm of a transitional serif with a clear lever for personality.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Sfizia in four styles, Regular, Italic, a second Italic variant and Bold, with the alternate italic carrying more pronounced display character. The tester exposes all four cuts so designers can audition body and display use side by side.

name: Sharphy
classification_notes: A humanist sans with sharp, slightly tapered terminals across seven weights from Thin to Bold, each with a true italic.
summary: A humanist sans from atipo with sharp terminals, fourteen styles across seven weights with matching italics.
description: Sharphy is a humanist sans from atipo foundry built around sharply cut, slightly tapered terminals that give the letterforms a crisp finish at large sizes while staying readable in body copy. The family runs across seven weights from Thin to Bold, each with a true italic, for fourteen styles in total. Suits brand systems, app interfaces and editorial work that wants warmth without softness, sitting between traditional humanist sans like Gill or Frutiger and the sharper contemporary cuts that have replaced them in identity work.
foundry_blurb: atipo list Sharphy in seven weights from Thin through ExtraLight, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold, each paired with a matching italic, giving fourteen styles in total. The tester exposes the full set across upright and italic axes.

name: Silka
classification_notes: A geometric sans with a generous lower-case x-height, eight weights from Thin to Black with matching italics. Pairs with Silka Mono.
summary: A geometric sans workhorse from atipo with eight weights and matching italics, paired with a companion Silka Mono.
description: Silka is one of atipo foundry's most-used releases, a geometric sans with a generous x-height that carries comfortably from interface text to large display lines. It runs across eight weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, which gives it the range to serve as a sole brand typeface for tech, editorial and lifestyle work. The family pairs with Silka Mono for code, captions and tabular data. Latin Extended support covers the major European languages.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Silka in sixteen styles, eight upright weights from Thin through Extra Light, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold, Bold and Black, each with a true italic. The tester exposes the full set of styles, and the foundry positions Silka Mono as the matching monospace companion.

name: Silka Mono
classification_notes: The monospaced companion to Silka, sharing its geometric construction and x-height. Eight weights from Thin to Black with matching italics.
summary: The monospaced companion to Silka, geometric in construction with eight weights and matching italics.
description: Silka Mono is the monospaced sibling to atipo foundry's Silka, sharing its geometric skeleton and generous x-height while fixing every glyph to a single width. It runs across eight weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, so it works as more than a code font, holding its own for captions, tabular data, technical labels and editorial pull-quotes. Use it as the structural counterpoint to Silka in a brand system, or on its own where the typewriter rhythm is the point.
foundry_blurb: atipo present Silka Mono in sixteen styles matching the Silka family, eight upright weights paired with italics. The page positions it as the monospaced companion to Silka, sharing the same proportional skeleton.

name: Stampa
classification_notes: A single-style stencil display face, drawn for posters, packaging and large-scale editorial use rather than text setting.
summary: A single-style stencil display face from atipo, made for posters, packaging and large editorial titling.
description: Stampa is a display stencil from atipo foundry, released as a single weight rather than a full system. The cut prioritises poster and packaging use, with letterforms broken by stencil bridges that read at distance and carry the rough, applied feel of stencilled signage. Best deployed for headlines, titling and identity moments where the medium itself is part of the message rather than for body copy.
foundry_blurb: atipo release Stampa as a single Regular weight, with the tester exposing only that one style. The page frames it as a display stencil rather than a multi-weight system.

name: Strawford
classification_notes: A geometric sans across seven weights from Thin to Black with matching italics. Sits in the same neighbourhood as Avenir and Gilroy, with slightly more humanist warmth.
summary: A geometric sans from atipo across seven weights with matching italics, sitting near Avenir and Gilroy with a touch more warmth.
description: Strawford is a geometric sans from atipo foundry built across seven weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, fourteen styles in total. The construction is clean and circular but softens the strict geometric model with slightly humanist proportions, giving it more warmth than a pure geometric for branding and product work. A solid choice for app interfaces, marketing sites, decks and editorial layouts where you want a friendly, contemporary sans with enough weight range to handle hierarchy on its own.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Strawford in fourteen styles, seven upright weights from Thin through Extra Light, Light, Regular, Medium, Bold and Black, each paired with a true italic.

name: Swiza
classification_notes: A slab serif across seven weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics. Slabs are bracketed, leaning more Egyptian than geometric.
summary: A bracketed slab serif from atipo across seven weights with matching italics, suited to editorial and brand systems.
description: Swiza is a slab serif from atipo foundry, sitting in the bracketed Egyptian slab tradition rather than the harder geometric slabs. It runs across seven weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, fourteen styles in total, which gives it real range for editorial body and display work. Suits long-read publications, brand systems wanting a confident voice, and pairings with a geometric sans like atipo's Silka or Strawford for a classic sans-and-slab combination.
foundry_blurb: atipo list Swiza in fourteen styles, seven upright weights from Thin through Extralight, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold, each with a true italic.

name: Wotfard
classification_notes: A geometric sans across seven weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, lighter and slightly more rounded than Strawford.
summary: A rounded geometric sans from atipo across seven weights with matching italics, a softer counterpart to Strawford.
description: Wotfard is a geometric sans from atipo foundry that softens the geometric model with slightly rounded terminals and a friendly lower-case. It runs across seven weights from Thin to Bold with matching italics, fourteen styles in total, and sits as a softer alternative to harder geometrics like Strawford in the same catalogue. A good pick for product brands, app interfaces, family and education contexts, and editorial work that wants a contemporary sans with a warmer character.
foundry_blurb: atipo offer Wotfard in fourteen styles, seven upright weights from Thin through Extralight, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold and Bold, each paired with a true italic.
